problem.summary: Compute one sum per segment for either fixed-width contiguous segments or ragged segments described by offsets. The baseline assigns one thread to each segment and scans sequentially. The optimized seed maps one block to each segment and performs a cooperative shared-memory reduction.
outputs: output[segment] contains the sum of input values in that segment
constraints: mode 0 uses fixed_segment_size and ignores offsets
optimized seed assumes one CTA can cover useful work per segment but loops for long ragged rows
floating-point addition order differs between baseline and optimized paths

verification.method: TODO: compare against CPU segmented sums and a hipCUB DeviceSegmentedReduce oracle for ragged offsets.

curation.notes: Template-only scaffold for fixed and ragged segmented reductions. Treat hipCUB/rocPRIM/hipCUB/rocThrust as the baseline, correctness oracle, and parts bin before claiming a custom win.
